failure_modes.text: Teams miss real system behavior when they judge each component separately and never inspect the interaction effects creating the whole.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the whole cannot be inferred from the parts alone.
failure_modes.mitigation: Inspect interaction effects between components: judging each part separately will miss the emergent behavior that matters.

failure_modes.text: Incremental part optimization can fail because the result is being produced by relationships between parts rather than by one weak component.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works when interaction effects matter more than component quality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Optimize for the relationships and feedback loops that produce the result, not for individual components in isolation.

failure_modes.text: Emergence becomes an excuse for drift when teams avoid setting the minimal structure and feedback needed for healthy pattern formation.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When “Let It Emerge” Becomes an Excuse for No Design.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set minimal structure and feedback mechanisms: emergence is not an excuse for drift; healthy pattern formation needs guardrails.

premortem_questions.text: What behavior only appears when these parts interact that we would miss if we scored them one by one?
premortem_questions.source_quote: To understand emergence, one must adopt systems thinking ... determine what emerges from that interaction.

premortem_questions.text: Which conditions, constraints, or feedback loops must be designed up front so better patterns can emerge instead of chaos?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when leaders must design conditions rather than script outcomes.

premortem_questions.text: Where are we mistaking implementation flexibility for permission to improvise without a supporting structure?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The focus on emergence can be misapplied as 'busking it'.

heuristics.text: Map interactions before optimizing components when the system-level result looks larger than the sum of the parts.
heuristics.source_quote: Emergence ... where complex outcomes arise from the interaction of simpler, distinct parts.

heuristics.text: Design enabling conditions and learning loops instead of pretending the full path can be scripted in advance.
heuristics.source_quote: Implementation is often more emergent than amenable to a blueprint.

heuristics.text: Use emergence to explain interaction-driven novelty, not to excuse absent design discipline.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger When “Let It Emerge” Becomes an Excuse for No Design.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks on interaction effects, condition design, and anti-busking discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader strategy-language sprawl beyond the clearest system and design moves.
open_questions: The source also supports creative synthesis, but the Wave 2 surface stays on emergence as a system behavior and design problem.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on systems-thinking, synthesis, and design-thinking.
antagonists: The source is strongest against optimization-theory style part-maximization and overly reductive decomposition.
structured_tensions: The clearest tensions are with optimization-theory and first-principles-thinking when reduction loses interaction effects.
